annotations.label: Probability of coin landing on edge

anchor.snippet: A computational model suggests that the chance of a coin landing on its edge and staying there is about 1 in 6,000 for an American nickel.

annotations.note: Empirical/physical estimate about real-world coin geometry; not the kind of statement formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Thickness-to-diameter ratio of fair three-sided coin

anchor.snippet: The ratio of the thickness of the coin to its diameter is thought to be between 0.577 and 0.866.

annotations.note: A physical/geometric estimate about three-sided fair coins, with no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Success probability of three-way flip

anchor.snippet: The three-way flip is 75% likely to work each time it is tried

annotations.note: An elementary probability calculation about a specific protocol; not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hand-flip bias (Diaconis)

anchor.snippet: in flipping by hand people generate a slight bias

annotations.note: Empirical claim about human-flipped coins; not a formal theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Same-side bias for caught coin

anchor.snippet: their theoretical analysis of the physics of coin tosses predicts a slight bias for a caught coin to be caught the same way up as it was thrown, with a probability of around 0.51

annotations.note: A physical/probabilistic result from rigid-body mechanics; no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Amsterdam 350,757-toss study

anchor.snippet: A much larger 2023 University of Amsterdam study

annotations.note: Reference to an empirical experiment; nothing to formalize in Mathlib.

annotations.label: TTH precedes THT twice as often

anchor.snippet: it is twice as likely that the triplet TTH will occur before THT than after it

annotations.note: Penney's-game style result; grep for TTH/THT/Penney in Mathlib found no relevant probability material.

annotations.label: THH precedes HHT three times as often

anchor.snippet: It is three times as likely that "THH will precede HHT" than that "THH will follow HHT"

annotations.note: Another Penney's-game pattern result; no occurrences of these triplet patterns or Penney's game in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coin flipping as Bernoulli process

anchor.snippet: The mathematical abstraction of the statistics of coin flipping is described by means of the Bernoulli process

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the single-trial `PMF.bernoulli` and `ProbabilityTheory.setBernoulli`, but a named `Bernoulli process` (i.i.d. sequence) is not present — grep finds no `bernoulliSeq`/`Bernoulli.process`.

annotations.label: Single flip as Bernoulli trial

anchor.snippet: a single flip of a coin is a Bernoulli trial

annotations.note: `PMF.bernoulli p h : PMF Bool` with `PMF.bernoulli_apply` directly formalizes a single Bernoulli trial.

annotations.label: Cryptographic coin-flip protocol

anchor.snippet: In telecommunications and cryptography , the following algorithm can be used

annotations.note: Cryptographic protocol description; Mathlib does not formalize coin-flipping commitment protocols.
